when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status and check the timing of the last dose, the agent should distinguish between no documented COVID-19 vaccination (no prior doses) and cases where a dose was given over 12 months ago. Only order a booster if there is documentation of a prior vaccine and the last dose was more than 12 months ago; do not order a booster if there is no record of any previous COVID-19 vaccination.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen and decide whether to order a new study, the agent should order a new CT Abdomen if the most recent procedure was performed more than 12 months ago OR if there is no record of any prior CT Abdomen; do not order a new study if the latest procedure was within the past 12 months.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status and order a booster if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, only order a booster if there is documentation of a prior COVID-19 vaccine and the last dose was over 12 months ago; do not order a booster if there is no evidence of any previous COVID-19 vaccination.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over a time period and there are no available heart rate observations for that period, the agent should respond explicitly that "no heart rate data is available for the specified period," rather than stating the average cannot be calculated, and should not attempt to calculate or report an average value.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over a specified time period, the agent should only use heart rate values recorded within that period and, if no data points exist in the period, explicitly state that "no heart rate data is available for the specified period" rather than reporting an average based on data outside the period or on a single value.

when asked to verify that a patient has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, the agent should first check for the presence and number of existing active orders; if none are found, create one, and if multiple active orders are present, discontinue all but the newest order—explicitly confirming the final state has only one active order.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over a specified time period, the agent should only include heart rate observations recorded within that time window; if no data points exist within the period, explicitly state "no heart rate data is available for the specified period" and do not report an average.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 or 12 hours, only include heart rate observations recorded within each specific time window; if there are no heart rate data points within a period, state "no heart rate data is available for the specified period" instead of reporting or assuming an average value.

when asked to determine the date of the last influenza vaccine and order a new one if it was administered more than 365 days ago, the agent should also order a new influenza vaccine if there is no record of any prior influenza vaccination—do not only act when a prior vaccine is found; treat missing vaccination records as needing a new order.

when asked to verify that a patient has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, the agent should first check the current number of active DVT prophylaxis orders; if there are zero, create one new active order, and if there are multiple, discontinue all but the newest so that only one remains—explicitly confirming the final state with exactly one active order.

when asked to verify that a patient has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, the agent should explicitly check for existing active orders before acting; if none are present, create one, but if multiple exist, discontinue all but the newest, and confirm that the patient ends up with exactly one active order.

when asked to verify that every active opioid analgesic order has a matching naloxone prescription, the agent should first check for any active opioid orders for the patient; if any are found, it must confirm that each has a corresponding active naloxone prescription, and if not, create a naloxone order for each unmatched opioid order—do not skip creating naloxone simply because no opioid orders are found, but always check and explicitly state the match or action taken.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and past 12 hours, if there are no heart rate observations within either time window, the agent should explicitly state "no heart rate data is available for the specified period" for each period, rather than saying the average is not available.

when asked to verify that every active opioid analgesic order has a matching naloxone prescription, the agent must first check for active opioid orders; if none are present, explicitly state that no naloxone prescription is needed, but if any are found, confirm that each has a corresponding naloxone prescription and create one if missing—do not skip this check or the naloxone order if opioids are present.

when asked to check if a urinary catheter has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order, the agent should first confirm that a catheter was placed (and not subsequently removed), then check the time since placement; if more than 48 hours have passed and there is no documented removal order, the agent must create a service request to remove the catheter—do not ask for clarification or wait for more information if these conditions are met.

when asked to check if a urinary catheter has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order, the agent must first verify that the catheter has not already been removed (i.e., no removal order exists after placement) before creating a removal service request; do not create a removal request if a removal order is already documented.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours, only include heart rate observations recorded within each specified time window; if there are no heart rate data points for a given period, explicitly state "no heart rate data is available for the specified period" for that window and do not report or assume an average value.

when asked to verify that a patient has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, always check for existing active orders first; if there are zero, create one, and if there are multiple, discontinue all but the newest—explicitly confirm and state in the response that there is exactly one active order after any actions taken. The main mistake was failing to confirm and report that only one active order remains at the end.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen and decide whether to order a new study, the agent must order a new CT Abdomen with IV contrast (indication 'Renal mass follow-up') if the most recent procedure was performed more than 12 months ago from the current date OR if there is no record of any prior CT Abdomen; do not skip the order if the last CT Abdomen date is missing, and always base the 12-month calculation on the true current date.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours, only include heart rate observations recorded within each specified time window; if there are no heart rate data points for a period, explicitly state "no heart rate data is available for the specified period" for that window and do not report or assume an average value.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status and order a booster if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, only order a booster if there is documentation of a prior COVID-19 vaccination and the last dose was over 12 months ago; do not order a booster if there is no record of any previous COVID-19 vaccination.

when asked to check if a urinary catheter has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order, always first verify that there is no documented removal order after the catheter was placed before creating a removal service request; do not create a removal request if a removal order already exists.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status and order a booster if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, only order a booster if there is documentation of a prior COVID-19 vaccination and the last dose was over 12 months ago; do not order a booster if there is no record of any previous COVID-19 vaccination.
